title: A-Team goal tree
description: One primitive: the goal. Milestones, tickets and code-level tasks differ only in altitude, not in kind. A goal is a leaf when its acceptance criteria are observable by a nameable check. Nodes are stored flat with parent pointers so the tree diffs cleanly in git.

id.description: dotted path, e.g. M-1, M-1.2, M-1.2.3 — position in the tree is readable from the id

outcome.description: one sentence: the observable end state, not the activity

criteria.description: every level has criteria. A parent's criteria are the integration check that its children being green does not guarantee.

leaf.description: true when every criterion carries a check with type != 'undecidable'

owner.description: human = the PO/PM must decide or perform this; the agent may not resolve it alone. Scan the tree for these: they are the control surface.

why_not_leaf.description: required when leaf=false: which criterion cannot be checked without further decomposition

depends_on.description: sibling or cousin ids that must complete first. Ordering, not decomposition.

statement.description: a claim that is true or false about the finished state. Not a task.

type.description: command = runs, exit code or output decides. ui = a human or screenshot observes a named element/state. process = a named flow is run end to end. measurement = a number crosses a stated threshold. undecidable = cannot be checked without decomposing; forces leaf=false.

how.description: the exact command, the exact screen and element, the exact flow, or the exact metric and threshold. Never 'verify that it works'.
